user.title: Choose a Flic button
user.description: Hold the button for at least 6 seconds to put it in public pairing mode, then choose it below.

confirm.title: Pair {name}
confirm.description: Pair the Flic button at {address} directly with Home Assistant. Flic v2 buttons are supported; legacy v1 buttons are not. Keep the button in public mode by holding it for at least 6 seconds immediately before submitting.

error.connection_failed: Home Assistant could not connect to the button. It may still be connected to a Flic Hub, be out of Bluetooth range, or no longer be available. Remove it from the hub, hold it for at least 6 seconds, and try again.
error.public_mode_required: The button did not respond in public pairing mode. Remove it from any Flic Hub, hold it for at least 6 seconds, and try again.
error.no_pairing_slots: The button has no free pairing slots. Remove it from one or more Flic Hubs or other controllers, then try again.
error.pairing_rejected: The button rejected the pairing request. Hold it for at least 6 seconds to re-enter public pairing mode, then try again.
error.authentication_failed: Home Assistant could not securely verify the button. Put it back in public pairing mode and try again. If this continues, reload the integration before retrying.
error.pairing_interrupted: The button stopped responding during secure pairing. Keep it near a Bluetooth proxy, hold it for at least 6 seconds, and try again.
error.initialization_timeout: Secure pairing completed, but the button did not finish initializing. Hold it for at least 6 seconds and try again.
error.pairing_failed: Pairing failed because the button returned an unexpected response. Put it back in public pairing mode and try again.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the Home Assistant logs.

abort.no_devices_found: No connectable supported Flic buttons are currently in public mode.
abort.not_supported: This Bluetooth device is not a supported Flic button. Flic v1 buttons are not supported.
